rulings.Case Title: JAY ELLIOT VS. DATA TRANSFER OF NEVADA, LLC, FORMERLY MIGO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 23, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANTS KENT HEYMAN, MAURICE GALLAGHER, ROBERT HALLIGAN, and, DATA TRANSFER, LLC DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action (fraudulent transfer.) Data Transfer is the "debtor" and received the assets. Data Transfer never "transferred" any assets within the meaning of CC section 3439.01(i). Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 3rd and 4th causes of action. Regarding unjust enrichment cause of action, plaintiff must plead a basis to void the contract and seek restitution. Regarding the UCL cause of action, plaintiff must specifically highlight the unlawful conduct and identify the legislative policy that supports the claim for unfair business practice. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ZORA L. KOLKEY VS. HEALTH NET FEDERAL SERVICES, INC. et al

rulings.Case Title: GRANT & WEBER, A CORPORATION VS. MAX FRUMES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Quash Or Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 23, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT MAX FRUMES Motion For Order To Quash or Vacate Judgment. Is denied. Defendant failed to comply with CCP section 473(b) by providing a copy of proposed responsive pleading with his moving papers. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PAYMON GHAFOURI et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 23, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to B&P Code section 17200 cause of action, no opposition. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave as to remaining causes of action. Plaintiff must allege tender of amount owed to maintain any cause of action for irregularity in the sale procedure. (See Abdallah vs. United Savings Bank (1996) 43 Cal.App. 4th 1101, 1109.) Breach of implied contract cause of action fails, mortgage can be created, renewed, or extended only by writing (CC section 2922.) No implied contract possible where a written contract exists. See Varni Bros. Corp. vs. Wine World, Inc. (1995) 35 Cal.App. 4th 880, 888-889.) The deed of trust governs the parties relationships. Plaintiff must plead facts with specificity to support the intentional tort/fraud and Foreclosure Prevention Act causes of action. Plaintiff also to plead timeliness of relief sought under CC section 2923.52. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE 1 et al VS. DOE 1, A UTAH CORPORATION et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA COUNTY*****
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 23, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT EDNA DOWELL MOTION TO TRANSFER. Motion granted. This personal injury action is transitory and not local. Defendant has right to have the case transferred to the County of her residence. Defendant has shown that no co-defendant resides in San Francisco. Incident did not occur in San Francisco. Case is transferred to Santa Clara County. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al VS. EILEEN BURKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 23, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT EILEEN BURKE Motion For Relief From Default. Motion denied without prejudice. Defendant does not provide sufficient legal reason under CCP section 473 to warrant relief from default. The press of business alone does not constitute excusable neglect. See Huh vs. Wang (2008) 158 Cal.App. 4th 1406, 1424. Defendant does not demonstrate diligence in attempting to set aside the default in view of the unexplained delay in filing this motion after the failure to achieve a stipulation. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NAVEEN SINGHA et al VS. NISSIM LANYADOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mo To Expunge Lis Pendes And For Atty Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 23, 2010, line 11, DEFENDANT NISSIM LANYADOO Motion To Expunge Lis Pendes and For Attorney Fees. Motion to expunge lis pendes is granted, request for attorney fees is denied. Complaint contains a real property claim for reconveyance. Plaintiffs fails to carry their burden to establish the probable validity of the reconveyance claim. An accounting at the time of foreclosure indicates a principal balance of $778,959 on the loan. No evidence supports any deduction applied to the interest on the note. Plaintiff admits Campbell is a licensed real estate broker, and evidence indicates Campbell arranged the loan and was paid to do so. Plaintiff admits receiving notice of trustee sale which warns Plaintiff is in default and unless he takes action, property may be sold. In light of plaintiff's beliefs about the note being satisfied, plaintiffs acted in substantial justification in recording lis pendens. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NAVEEN SINGHA et al VS. NISSIM LANYADOO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 23, 2010, line 11, DEFENDANT NISSIM LANYADOO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 3rd cause of action for injunctive relief. Plaintiffs to plead proper basis for injunction. Demurrer overruled as to 5th cause of action. Court's ruling on TRO and preliminary injunction is not determinative of whether plaintiffs have properly alleged facts sufficient to support this claim. =(302/CWW)
